name: Russian Federation — Strategic Leadership KPV Assessment (Open Source)
description: Open-source KPV assessment of Russian strategic leadership. AO INVERTED: high AO = visible to allied OSINT. All data from public sources: Moscow Times, Meduza, Bellingcat, RUSI, IISS, CRS, think tank analyses. No classified sources.
ao_note: INVERTED AO: High AO = clearly visible to allied OSINT. Low AO = opaque — indicates intelligence gap.

roles.title: Vladimir Putin — President / Supreme Commander
roles.notes: Hypercentralised authority; succession architecture fragmented (Mishustin, Patrushev, Medvedev each hold partial successor claim). KPCI=12 in compound KPV terms. Source: Kremlin public statements; PONARS Eurasia; CRS R46273.

roles.title: Nikolai Patrushev — Presidential Aide / Former NSC Secretary
roles.notes: FSB/SVR relationship capital; national security architecture tacit knowledge. Longest-serving member of Putin inner circle. Source: Kremlin public appointments; Meduza; RUSI analysis.

roles.title: Director, Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR)
roles.notes: HUMINT and illegals programme. Naryshkin publicly identified; operational network tacit. Source: SVR public announcements; Bellingcat Salisbury/Skripal analysis; CRS.

roles.title: Director, FSB
roles.notes: Domestic CI and offensive cyber (18th Centre). Bortnikov publicly identified. Source: FSB public announcements; Microsoft MSTIC; CISA advisories (Sandworm attribution).

roles.title: FSB 18th Centre Director (Sandworm / GRU Cyber)
roles.notes: INTELLIGENCE GAP: AO=1.5. Offensive cyber operational directors are opaque. Sandworm attributed to GRU Unit 74455 at entity level only. Source: CISA AA22-110A (public); DOJ indictments; Microsoft MSTIC.

roles.title: Director General, Rosatom
roles.notes: Likhachev publicly identified. Dual civil/weapons nuclear programme. International nuclear partnership relationships (Belt and Road nuclear). Source: Rosatom public org; WNA; CRS RL34248.

roles.title: Rosatom — Nuclear Weapons Complex Director
roles.notes: INTELLIGENCE GAP: AO=2.0. VNIIEF (Sarov) and VNIITF weapons complex directors are publicly associated with institutes but individual design authority is opaque. Source: NTI Russia profile; SIPRI; public Russian defence literature.

roles.title: Deputy PM for Military-Industrial Complex (Borisov/Belousov)
roles.notes: Military-industrial coordination. Publicly identified role. Source: Russian Government public org; RUSI Defence Procurement analysis.

roles.title: CEO, Rostec (Sergei Chemezov)
roles.notes: Rostec: Russia's defence industrial conglomerate. Chemezov is sanctioned and publicly identified. S-400, Kalashnikov, aircraft engine production. Source: Rostec public org; OFAC SDN list (public); IISS.

roles.title: Director, GRU (Military Intelligence)
roles.notes: GRU Director publicly known. Unit-level attribution (APT28/Fancy Bear = Unit 26165) established via DOJ indictments. Source: DOJ indictments (public); Bellingcat.

roles.title: Chief of General Staff (Gerasimov)
roles.notes: Gerasimov publicly identified. 'Gerasimov doctrine' (hybrid warfare) substantially tacit as implemented. Source: Russian MOD public org; RUSI; Brookings.
